title: Agent Health Monitoring
dataSources: EDR agent status API, last check-in timestamps
spl:
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:sensor_health"
| eval hours_since_checkin=round((now()-last_seen_epoch)/3600,1)
| where hours_since_checkin > 24 OR sensor_version < "6.50"
| table hostname, os, sensor_version, hours_since_checkin, status
description: Endpoints without healthy EDR agents are blind spots. Gap detection ensures comprehensive coverage.
value: Endpoints without healthy EDR agents are blind spots. Gap detection ensures comprehensive coverage.
implementation: Poll EDR agent status API daily. Identify agents offline >24 hours, outdated versions, or degraded status. Cross-reference with CMDB for full coverage analysis. Alert on critical servers with unhealthy agents.
visualization: Table (unhealthy agents), Single value (% healthy), Pie chart (agent version distribution), Bar chart (offline by OS).
knownFalsePositives: Planned rollouts, travel laptops, golden images, and decommissioned assets can look like a missing agent; use the product’s own health or device list before you treat it as an attack.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: EDR TA, scripted input.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: EDR agent status API, last check-in timestamps.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll EDR agent status API daily. Identify agents offline >24 hours, outdated versions, or degraded status. Cross-reference with CMDB for full coverage analysis. Alert on critical servers with unhealthy agents.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:sensor_health"
| eval hours_since_checkin=round((now()-last_seen_epoch)/3600,1)
| where hours_since_checkin > 24 OR sensor_version < "6.50"
| table hostname, os, sensor_version, hours_since_checkin, status
```

Understanding this SPL

**Agent Health Monitoring** — Endpoints without healthy EDR agents are blind spots. Gap detection ensures comprehensive coverage.

Documented **Data sources**: EDR agent status API, last check-in timestamps. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): EDR TA, scripted input. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: edr; **sourcetype**: crowdstrike:sensor_health. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=edr, sourcetype="crowdstrike:sensor_health". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_since_checkin** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where hours_since_checkin > 24 OR sensor_version < "6.50"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Agent Health Monitoring**): table hostname, os, sensor_version, hours_since_checkin, status


Step 3 — Validate
In the EDR or sensor vendor console, compare last check-in, version, and status for the same hosts in this result set. Staging, travel, and retired assets often explain “offline” or old versions; confirm against CMDB and ownership before treating as a security failure.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unhealthy agents), Single value (% healthy), Pie chart (agent version distribution), Bar chart (offline by OS).
grandmaExplanation: We check that laptops and servers are really checking in to their security sensor so a broken install does not leave a wide-open hole for attackers.
